provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'Yesterday, I saw a huge aeroplane in the sky.'
provenance.note: 'wings' glossed (above level); needed to make the definition unambiguous vs spaceship.

items.d: a big thing with wings that carries people high in the sky
items.s: Yesterday, I saw a huge ___ in the sky.
items.hintDe: Damit fliegen Menschen über die Wolken.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.62, 'An alien was next to him.'

items.d: a living thing that is not from Earth
items.s: A green ___ came to Earth from space.
items.hintDe: Dieses Wesen kommt von einem anderen Planeten.

provenance.sbRef: WB p.66, 'He wanted to warn the boss of the Space Centre.'

items.d: the man or woman you work for
items.s: My mum has a new ___ at the office.
items.hintDe: Diese Person ist bei der Arbeit über dir.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'Benson connected a cable to his spacesuit.'

items.d: a long thing that connects two machines
items.s: Benson connected a ___ to his spacesuit.
items.hintDe: Damit verbindest du zwei Geräte.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.65, 'Calm down, Gillian. You're safe here with us.'
provenance.note: Fixed phrase; distractors are wrong forms/phrases, never the headword.

items.d: you call this out to a friend who is too excited or scared
items.s: ___! There is nothing to be afraid of.
items.hintDe: Das sagst du zu jemandem, der zu aufgeregt ist.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'Vienna is the capital of Austria.'

items.d: the city of a country where the king or queen lives
items.s: Vienna is the ___ of Austria.
items.hintDe: Die wichtigste Stadt eines Landes.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'The chair was very comfortable!'

items.d: nice to be on and you are not tired
items.s: The chair was very ___ and I did not want to get up.
items.hintDe: So fühlt sich ein weicher Sessel an.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.62, story 'Benson's bad luck'

items.d: the boss of a spaceship who gives the crew their jobs
items.s: The ___ told the crew what to do.
items.hintDe: Diese Person hat auf dem Raumschiff das Sagen.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'Benson has to save his crew on the spaceship.'

items.d: all the people who work on a ship or a plane
items.s: Benson wants to help his ___ on the spaceship.
items.hintDe: Alle, die auf einem Schiff zusammenarbeiten.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'She's an expert.'

items.d: a man or woman who is very good at one thing
items.s: She studied space for many years, so she is an ___.
items.hintDe: Jemand, der sich mit einer Sache richtig gut auskennt.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.63 song, 'Commander Benson's our hero.'

items.d: a great man people love and thank for what he did
items.s: Commander Benson is our ___ - we all love and thank him.
items.hintDe: So nennt man jemanden, der etwas Mutiges und Gutes getan hat.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'The news isn't true. It's a hoax.'

items.d: a false story that people are told is true
items.s: The news isn't true. It's a ___.
items.hintDe: Eine Lüge, die echt aussehen soll.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'In that case, we should give him morphing powers.'
provenance.note: Fixed phrase; distractors are wrong but plausible phrases, headword never present.

items.d: if that is so, then
items.s: You don't like fish? ___, let's have chicken.
items.hintDe: Wenn das so ist, dann ...

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'The police started their investigation yesterday.'

items.d: the work the police do to find out what really happened
items.s: The police did an ___ to find out what happened.
items.hintDe: Damit findet die Polizei heraus, was wirklich passiert ist.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.63, 'He found a gold key on the ground.'

items.d: a small thing that opens a door or a car
items.s: He put the ___ in the door and opened it.
items.hintDe: Damit sperrst du eine Tür auf.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.62, 'The alien gave him a little machine.'

items.d: a thing that does a job for you, like washing clothes
items.s: Benson used a ___ to talk to the alien.
items.hintDe: Ein Gerät, das eine Arbeit für dich erledigt.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'I'm sorry, but this is just nonsense.'

items.d: talk that is mad and not true
items.s: You're talking ___! That story can't be true.
items.hintDe: Wenn jemand etwas Dummes sagt, das nicht stimmt.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'My grandma showed me an old photograph.'

items.d: a picture you take to keep
items.s: My grandma showed me a ___ of her sister.
items.hintDe: Ein Bild, das du mit der Kamera machst.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.62, 'We are travellers from the planet Arconia.'

items.d: a big thing in space where people or aliens can live, like Earth or Mars
items.s: Earth is the ___ where we all live.
items.hintDe: Die Erde und der Mars sind je so ein Himmelskörper.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'There are lots of stars and planets out there in space.'

items.d: everything far away from Earth, where the stars and planets are
items.s: There are lots of stars and planets out there in ___.
items.hintDe: Dort draußen sind die Sterne und Planeten.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.63, 'He went to the old Space Centre on Earth.'

items.d: the place on Earth where people work on spaceships
items.s: We visited the ___ and looked at the spaceships.
items.hintDe: Ein Ort auf der Erde, von dem aus man ins Weltall startet.

items.d: a ship that travels in space
items.s: A ___ is a ship that travels in space.
items.hintDe: Damit fliegt man durch das Weltall.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.62, 'Benson connected a cable to his spacesuit.'

items.d: the clothes you wear out in space
items.s: Benson wears a ___ outside the spaceship.
items.hintDe: Diese Kleidung schützt dich draußen im Weltall.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'There's a statue of Lord Nelson at Trafalgar Square.'

items.d: a big thing made of stone that looks like a famous man
items.s: There's a ___ of Lord Nelson at Trafalgar Square.
items.hintDe: Ein großes Standbild aus Stein, oft von einer berühmten Person.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'You have to connect the cable to the spacesuit.'

items.d: to join two machines with a cable
items.s: You have to ___ the cable to the spacesuit.
items.hintDe: Zwei Dinge mit einem Kabel zusammenbringen.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'In the film, aliens try to destroy Earth.'

items.d: to break a place so much that nothing is there
items.s: In the story, aliens want to ___ the town and all the buildings.
items.hintDe: Etwas so kaputt machen, dass nichts übrig bleibt.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'I think aliens want to kidnap people from Earth.'

items.d: to take people away and keep them, often for money
items.s: I think aliens want to ___ people from Earth.
items.hintDe: Einen Menschen mit Gewalt wegbringen und festhalten.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'Benson went outside to repair the broken spaceship.'

items.d: to make a thing work again after it breaks
items.s: Benson went outside to ___ the spaceship.
items.hintDe: Etwas Kaputtes wieder ganz machen.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'The aliens want to take over the Earth.'

items.d: to become the new boss of a place and rule it
items.s: The aliens want to ___ the Earth.
items.hintDe: Ein Land oder einen Ort unter seine Kontrolle bringen.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'I'm a traveller from planet Arconia.'

items.d: a man or woman who travels from place to place
items.s: I'm a ___ from planet Arconia.
items.hintDe: Jemand, der von Ort zu Ort unterwegs ist.

provenance.sbRef: SB p.64, 'UFOs - are they really out there?'

items.d: a thing in the sky that some people think is from space
items.s: We saw a ___ in the sky last night, but nobody knew what it was.
items.hintDe: Ein Flugobjekt, das niemand erklären kann.

provenance.sbRef: masterlist exampleSb: 'It's good to have you as a visitor.'

items.d: a man or woman who comes to a place to see it
items.s: Today we had a ___ from England who told us about London.
items.hintDe: Jemand, der zu Besuch kommt.
